CL-01.title: Website Security Certificate
CL-01.description: The website does not have a valid SSL certificate, exposing user data to security risks.
CL-01.remediation: Install and configure a valid SSL certificate to encrypt the data transmitted between the user and the server.

CL-11.title: Password Security
CL-11.description: The website allows weak password creation without enforced security measures, making it vulnerable to brute force attacks.
CL-11.remediation: Implement password strength requirements and enable multi-factor authentication for enhanced security.

CL-20.title: GDPR Compliance
CL-20.description: The website lacks a GDPR compliance statement and explicit user consent mechanisms for data processing.
CL-20.remediation: Implement GDPR-compliant privacy policies, cookie consent, and data processing notifications.

CL-01.title: Ensure HTTPS Protocol is Enforced
CL-01.description: Ensure that the website enforces the use of HTTPS protocol to secure data transmission.

CL-02.title: Implement Cross-Origin Resource Sharing (CORS) Protection
CL-02.description: Ensure that Cross-Origin Resource Sharing protection is implemented to prevent unauthorized access to resources.

CL-03.title: Enable Content Security Policy (CSP)
CL-03.description: Implement Content Security Policy to mitigate risks associated with content injection attacks.

CL-04.title: Conduct Regular Security Audits
CL-04.description: Regularly audit the website for security vulnerabilities and address them promptly.
CL-04.remediation: Implement a routine security audit schedule.

CL-01.title: SSL/TLS Usage
CL-01.description: Website must use SSL/TLS to encrypt data transmission.

CL-02.title: Content Security Policy
CL-02.description: Website should have a proper Content Security Policy in place.
CL-02.remediation: Implement a Content Security Policy to prevent XSS attacks.

CL-03.title: Two-Factor Authentication
CL-03.description: Strongly recommended to have Two-Factor Authentication for user accounts.
